health.s: 4 CPSC recalls (1 in past 24 months); latest hazard: the connection between the yeti link expansion modules and the cables can overhe.

topHazards.label: The connection between the Yeti Link Expansion modules and the cables can overheat and melt, posing fire and burn hazards.
The magnet-lined closures can fail and result in detached magnets, posing a risk of serious injury or death if ingested. When two or more high-powered magnets …
The magnet slider on the lid can eject and hot contents can spill out, posing injury and burn hazards.
The brakes on the snow bike can fail after the conversion kit has been installed, posing a crash hazard to the user.

sampleRecalls.title: Goal Zero Recalls Yeti Link Modules with EC8 Cables Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Yeti Link Expansion Modules along with EC8 power cables. They were sold as kits and also sold separately. The modules and cables were used in homes and installed in vehicles. For vehicles, the module allows consumers to wire the modules to the vehicle alternator to enable the battery to charge whi…
hazards: The connection between the Yeti Link Expansion modules and the cables can overheat and melt, posing fire and burn hazards.
injuries: The firm has received two reports of the cables melting inside a vehicle, causing minor property damage. No injuries have been reported.
products: Yeti Link Expansion Modules and EC8 Cables
units: About 34,460 (In addition, about 815 were sold in Canada) (The EC8 cables were previously recalled in April 2021)

sampleRecalls.title: YETI Recalls 1.9 Million Soft Coolers and Gear Cases Due to Magnet Ingestion Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the YETI Hopper M30 Soft Cooler 1.0 and 2.0, Hopper M20 Soft Backpack Cooler and SideKick Dry Gear Case. The products have a main-pocket closure lined with high-powered magnets, which are enclosed in a heat-sealed plastic strip. The M20 Soft Backpack Cooler measures about 18 inches wide and 18 inc…
hazards: The magnet-lined closures can fail and result in detached magnets, posing a risk of serious injury or death if ingested. When two or more high-powered magnets …
injuries: The firm has received 1,399 reports of the magnet-lined closures degrading or failing, including reports of missing or detached magnets. No magnet ingestions or injuries have been reported.
products: YETI soft coolers and gear cases
units: About 1.9 million (In addition, about 40,760 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: YETI Recalls Rambler Travel Mugs with Stronghold Lid Due to Injury and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the Rambler 20 oz Travel Mug with Stronghold Lid. The travel mugs were sold in a variety of colors. The recall only involves travel mugs with the date code 34204010. The date code is located on the bottom of the travel mug base. The SKU is located on the product packaging slip for online orders, a…
hazards: The magnet slider on the lid can eject and hot contents can spill out, posing injury and burn hazards.
injuries: The firm has received two reports of the magnetic slider on the Rambler 20 oz Travel Mug with Stronghold Lid being ejected after being sealed with hot liquid. No injuries have been reported.
products: Rambler® 20 oz Travel Mugs with Stronghold Lids

sampleRecalls.title: Camso Recalls Yeti SnowMX Conversion Kits Due to Crash Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Yeti Snow MX kits used to convert dirt bike motorcycles into snow bikes for use in snow. The kit allows the replacement of the front wheel of a motorbike with a ski and rear suspension and wheels with a track system, and the addition of appropriate brakes with other components. The Yeti Snow MX ki…
hazards: The brakes on the snow bike can fail after the conversion kit has been installed, posing a crash hazard to the user.
injuries: Camso has received 12 incidents of the brakes failing. No injuries have been reported.
